description: Our overall experience with eLab Journal has been exceptional. We are glad we chose to partner with them because they were definitely the right choice! They have helped us immensely and we would not have achieved a successful project outcome without them.
author: Maura M.
pros: Working with the team at eLab Journal to implement our LIMS and make enhancements as our laboratory has grown has been a wonderful experience. Everyone we have worked with is responsive and collaborative. The software itself has amazing functionality and provides the features we need to act as an efficient, high throughput testing laboratory.
cons: When we implemented the software in 2020, some of the menu items and navigation seemed disjointed and not exceptionally intuitive. eLab Journal has evolved since then and we have seen improvements. The team is also open to our feedback as system users.

author: Helene K.
pros: Easy to use, modular, everything is tracable
cons: Takes some time to set it up at first, but then you quickly get a lot of benefits

review_title: Bioraft / SciShield is a Chemical Compliance Software That Fails to Deliver
description: Bioraft - Scishield is a very low-quality system with no SDS libraries, no real-time inventory tracking, no automated GSH, haz class reporting, and poor customer technical support. Offering a simple download of an Excel spreadsheet that the user enters with no function defeats the purpose of having software in the first place.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Lightweight Basic Functionality (very basic) Ability to upload training data
cons: Bioraft - Scishield is a low-quality system for chemical inventory management required by laboratories, industries, and organizations that deal with hazardous materials and will need to rely on accurate chemical tracking, hazards, and safety. Cons: The software 1) Bioraft - SciShield is one of the lowest-quality chemical inventory management systems on the market. 2) Bioraft - SciShield does not facilitate regulatory compliance due to inability to generate detailed reports, track expiration dates, and ensure proper storage. It lacks integration with other essential systems and safety compliance databases. 3) Bioraft - SciShield has poorly designed menus and unintuitive controls and is outdated and clunky. 4) Bioraft - Scishield has no link to safety data sheets other than a link to Google search, no SDS library management, and offers a simple downloaded CSV of what each lab manually entered. 5) Bioraft - Scishield provides no information in database format to download a list of your chemicals, with corresponding hazard classes, GSH codes, pictograms, proper cabinet container storage, or sortable queries based on any criteria. 6) Bioraft- Scishield, has bugs, fails to save data, often can not add chemicals due some type of error, and it is very clear the developers care very little about delivering a functional product offering insufficient customer support. Cons: Customer Support A frustrating software often comes with poor customer support. Here concerns are not taken seriously, and suggestions are not welcome. The technical representative was dismissive, unhelpful, and he lacked basic knowledge about the issue and its software. To make matters worse, no return phone calls, menu options on call in , etc etc. Universities should invest in systems that are essential for ensuring accuracy, safety, and long-term efficiency. This is not one of them.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Helps track and manage training, protocols, and regulatory requirements, reducing compliance gaps and generally intuitive for researchers and lab staff once set up.
cons: Can be expensive, especially for smaller labs or projects with limited funding and may feel restrictive for labs with highly customized workflows.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Would never go back to a paper based system now.
author: Angela R.
pros: Easy to use, traceable and auditable. Helps planning, organisation and tracking of work.
cons: Labelling of images, gel images for example, is tricky and much easier to do in other packages and bring in the images already labelled.

description: This has been a great experience. At first had only planned to use for single aspect of research project and now pushing for us to incorporate into other areas. Customer support has been excellent. Always quick to respond when have questions or requests, and so very willing to work with us to customize the system to our needs.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Although there was a learning process to using the system, it did not take more than a week for us to build the framework that needed for managing samples and recording experimental data. Have multiple users with different levels of comfort with technology and hearing more and more from group that so glad that set this up for this research project. Really appreciate that Microsoft Office integrated into system, not only for editing files but as sections within a entry. This has allowed users that do not have access to office to work with us with ease
cons: Lacking some options that know are available on other electronic notebook freeware (To Do Lists, Task assignment, Calendar, Messaging center, view only of integrating Microsoft office components, comment tracking, tags for indexing entries). One option that would love to have that seen in other electronic notebook software is the option to share study with outside user so can view (not edit) for defined period of time without using a licensed space. Would be happy if there was a limit on how many of these could exist at a time

author: Hannah C.
pros: Handling a large inventory of samples and reagents used to be a challenge--until we implemented eLabNext. The barcode label printing and scanning capabilities have streamlined our workflows and minimized human errors. What I appreciate most is the seamless integration of inventory items with out experiments and protocols, keeping everything organized and easily accessible. The eLabNext team provided outstanding support during onboarding, ensuring a smooth and stress-free transition. This platform has become an essential tool for our lab.
cons: There was/is never a bad experience with eLabNext and the team.

review_title: Excellent for organization - at a cost
author: Mackenzie M.
pros: I really enjoy using SciSure for inventory management primarily. It's very easy to upload and organize samples, especially if you're working with multiple studies with many samples.
cons: It is an expensive platform, a small lab may not be able to afford it. It does take a while to get set up and plan everything accordingly.

description: Great, easy to use. We don't use any paper labjournals anymore.
author: Francisca V.
pros: This ELN is easy to inplement en very easy to use. Customer support is great.
cons: Our internal servers are not connected to the ELN.

review_title: Digitalization of Manufacturing Processes at Viralgen using eLabJournal
author: Xabier U.
pros: When using eLabJournal we take advantage of: - We no longer use paper-based experiments. - Instead of having different repositories for experiment and sample results, everything is stored in a single digital repository, avoiding the exchange of mails with these results. - The Audit Trail of the samples is really good. - You can trace the Samples that are used in an experiment really easily. - Sample reception process using QR has simplified this process within Viralgen. - The ingestion of data from third party software using API is straightforward. - The implementation of eLAbJournal has allowed more communication between laboratories and departments. - The integration with other third-party applications is possible using the API. In our case with SAP.
cons: - When being used to paper-based experiments, the investment of time you need to digitalize your experiments in eLabJournal is really high. - We miss some Project/Study/Experiment filtering tools.

description: Elabjournal enforces high-quality documentation and the rigid structure of Project > Study > Experiment with the definition of aims and sub-aims helps to break complex problems into simpler tasks.
author: Georg M.
pros: In my personal experience, elabjournal did not only improve the structure of my documentation, it also improved experiment planning, design and execution.
cons: table or list based viewing and editing can be a pain. E.g. entering many new samples to the inventory for largely overlapping yet different properties is a time consuming task.

review_title: 2-3 Things that NEED to be implemented into elab
author: Mitchell Z.
pros: I like that you can edit samples easily, and that you have a lot of possibilities with the configuration-map
cons: you need to implement 3 functions:1. implement "personalised-view" of your samples everywhere! at the moment, we only have personalised view on the "sample-list". We truly need to have a personalised view in "Advanced-Search-Results", as well as in each specific Location.2. Possibilities to change Date-format. = we sort our Dates with this format: DD/MM/YYYY -> elab should register this and when we try sorting by date, it should start with sorting by YYYY. At the moment, it is only Sorting by DD, which is absolutely not usable for us.3. Allow Control-C/Selection of Search-Results in Sample-List (or/and advanced-Search, but only when number 1 has been adapted too) = this would allow us to rapidly export our search-results from sample-list to excel etc.

review_title: eLab - easy to use and effective
author: David G.
pros: quick integration and setting up the zebra printer only took seconds
cons: I would use same name for experiement, project, and sometimes study.

description: We leverage this tool to enable our IP documentation and trade secret processes. It is critical in our valuation and assessment of multiple processes across multiple species. It allows researchers to easily move between projects and scales well as our operations grow.
author: Tania S.
pros: This product enables us to ensure a secure environment for our IP related data. It is also seamless across our different labs and allows us to easily move between species and topics. The tool has also been easy to accommodate our rapid growth in researchers over the years.
cons: There are some features like stylus, etc. that would be nice to have. But overall it meets the majority of our needs.

author: Julien S.
pros: Comprehensive software with experiment, sample and equipment management. Very practical for small or large companies
cons: Sometimes a little slow to set up at first.

review_title: Very handy electronic lab journal software that will save you lots of time
author: Ruth L.
pros: Ease of use Protocol management Template possibility to save time Interactive interface Fast customer service
cons: Some features could be improved (e.g. more options for the text sections)

review_title: Overall good with a few hiccups
description: One of the better options that I have tried
author: Laura K.
pros: Easy to use overall, ability to track cells history throughout experiments, template applications
cons: Needs better software integration. Excel constantly doesn’t allow my formulas to Calculate. Easier inventory access and ability to delete or add into checked out spots

description: Undecided if its an improvement or not. The jury is still out.
author: Andy P.
pros: Not sure really havnt started using it properly yet, taken a lot of time to sort out the infrastructure, would be easier if the Elab team has better insight into the peripherals and things needed to start using the software effectively
cons: Very slow to get up and running-this is mainly due to limited home resource and nothing to do with the product. However Eppendorff could start to sell printers and labels etc so they cover everything you need to start embedding the software effectively
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: eLabJournal has been a valuable addition to our company. It has allowed us to move away from endless spreadsheets. We are able to keep all pertinent project information organized and easily searchable. Using the inventory function is making out chemical, equipment and sample inventory a breeze.
author: Kari B.
pros: Each section is easily customizable to fit our company's needs. I like the ability to keep everything neatly organized and easy to search.
cons: I would love to see the export report function be able to turn an excel spreadsheet into a readable pdf. It currently does not allow for that function.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Yashna W.
pros: The ease of use and organization is the best feature
cons: No issue was found as of now. The only thing can be said is initially it was a bit complicated

review_title: Essential data and inventory management tool
author: Miles M.
pros: The integration with eLabJournal makes this an essential part of daily workflows, tracking samples by lineage and attaching them to experiments. The layout is very intuitive, and customer support for more advanced projects has been really amazing.
cons: It might be nice if info for reagents could be imported directly from vendor websites.

author: Alisson B.
pros: I like that eLabJournal was really easy to implement in the regular lab work
cons: It could be a bit more personalized on how the experiments can be viewed on list mode

author: Jessie B.
pros: I really like how it is easy to modify and add sections in an entry.
cons: I don't like filling out a table. It's hard to move down a column since the only way to do it is by clicking into each box.

review_title: Using elabjournal by a technician as a regular tool in the lab
author: Martin G.
pros: Monitoring sheets are all secured and better tracked
cons: Nothing really, it’s easy to use and user friendly

author: Kannan G.
pros: I have used eLab Journal for nearly two years, it has very good user interface and easy options to maintain a electronic lab journal. it has handy app that you can upload everything from your mobile and access your data in your laptop! you can also keep track of your samples, location, share data with other users etc.
cons: Although it has user friendly interface, it still needs some improvement to make it more user friendly.
